Symptom

The integration framework (B1i) does not process messages and the number of messages in queues does not change or is increasing. This behavior may have consequences like:

 

  • for Intercompany solution, data replication, for example business partners, items, exchange rate, global transactions, doesn’t work as expect.

  • for SAP Business One mobile app, the “Processing failed” issue happens.

  • for Electronic Invoice, the invoice is in “Waiting for processing” status.

 

Note:

  • For the case that messages from external systems are sent to the integration framework faster that the integration framework processes them, the number of messages in queues also increases, but there are no messages that are blocking queues. To improve the integration framework performance, refer to SAP Note 2192737.

  • For the case that the events are not sent to integration framework or the events are filtered, refer to the SAP Note 1882000 for solutions. For a scenario step triggered by an event, the step will run after the event is received by the integration framework and dispatched to the relevant scenario step.

Reproducing the issue

Scenario:

  1. In the integration framework, choose MONITORING → Queue Monitor,.

    You observe that the number of messages is not changing or increasing.

  2. In the integration framework, choose TOOLS → Control Center → Monitoring → Execution Status.
    No transaction is displayed or the transactions displayed do not disappear after some time.

Solution

This document contains the following information and troubleshooting tips:

  1. Troubleshooting methodology 
  2. General process for troubleshooting
  3. Useful information
  4. Report an SAP incident

 

﻿1. Troubleshooting methodology

1.1  Queue Monitor

Target: monitor the queue processing and delete the messages when necessary.

Processing of asynchronous scenario steps consists of multiple internal process steps. The process steps hand over the message using the inbuilt queuing mechanism of the integration framework.

 (Screenshot – 1: Queuing Mechanism)

(Screenshot – 2: Queue Monitor)

You can identify internal queues of the integration framework by the queue name (Screen 2-1) and stream name (Screen 2-2). A stream is a sub queue. The queue monitor allows you to check the current status in the internal queue.

(Screenshot – 3: Message)

You can Display (Screen 2-4) or Delete (Screen 2-5) messages in a queue or stream. In case you need to delete all of the messages for one queue, click the Clear button (Screen 2-6). Open the message to check the time stamp to identify if it is old message or not by using like MessageId (Screenshot 3-1) / BeginTimeStamp (Screenshot 3-2).

Note:

  • Deleting/Clearing messages from the queue can result in data loss or incomplete processing. Ensure that the data being deleted is not required and/or can be sent from the sender system again.

  • The count of the messages in the stream doesn’t indicate the queue status clearly, monitor the sequence (Screen 2-3) of the messages as well to confirm the queue is moving or not.

  • Delete/Clear will pop up a new window. Wait until the window is closed automatically. The automatic closing of the window could take some time due to the resource lock/recovery. In case it takes much time without completion, restart the SAP Business One Integration Service and delete the message(s) again. If the issue persists, refer to the solution in the SAP Note 2244525.

1.2  IPO Step Status

Target: no deactivated IPO Steps except the IPO steps deactivated by default.

 (Screenshot – 4: IPO Step Status)

To check the IPO step status, choose Tools → Control Center → Monitoring → IPO Step Status, select the Deactivated Steps Only and Exception on Last Execution Only checkboxes and click Submit.

 (Screenshot – 5: IPO Step Status)

  • If IPO steps (Screen 5-1) are deactivated (Active=false. Screen 5-2) due to an exception and the cause (Screen 5-3) is not deployment, click the (Re-)Activate button to reactivate the IPO step. The steps in the screenshot above are deactivated by default which is normal. We see Cause = deployment for these steps. Due to the different integration framework versions, the number of default deactivated steps may vary.
  • If the IPO step is continuously deactivated, the first message that waits in the queue is most likely causing problems. Refer to the chapter 1.1 to delete the message.
  • If IPO steps are deactivated due to recovery (Cause = recovery) and RecoveryDo (chapter 1.4) is running in the integration framework, (check MONITORING → Execution Status).  Wait for some minutes until RecoveryDo is processed. IPO steps are activated automatically. If no RecoveryDo is running, activate the IPO steps or restart the integration framework service to trigger recovery. After the IPO steps deactivated, there should be a detailed error message in the Last Exception which indicates what causes the IPO steps deactivated.

    • For DI errors, refer to the SAP Notes 2360911 and 2057143 for troubleshooting.

    • For other errors, the cause could be related to runtime issues, for example, invalid XML, connection closed due to the network. The issue should be manually resolved if retrying via recovery fails. For some general technical errors, refer to the SAP Note 2130388.

1.3  TID Panel

Target: ensure that there are no in-doubt transactions listed.

In-doubt transaction (Screenshot 6-1): STARTED/INCOMMIT/INROLLBACK.

Final transaction (Screenshot 6-2): CANCALLED/COMPLETED.

 (Screenshot – 6: TID Panel)

To check in-doubt transactions, choose TOOLS → Control Center → Monitoring → TID-Panel, select the "STARTED" / "INCOMMIT" / “INROLLBACK” status, and click Submit. 

 (Screenshot – 7: TID Panel)

 

If transactions are displayed:

  • restart the integration framework service to trigger recovery. Wait for some minutes until recovery is completed. For more information, refer to section 2.

  • in case the recovery doesn’t work, evaluate the transaction by using IPO Log button (Screenshot 7-2) and according to the time stamp (Screenshot 7-1) which indicates the transactions are not processed for a long time, consider deleting the transaction by using Delete button (Screenshot 7-3). In case there is any technical error in the IPO Log, refer to the SAP Note 2130388 for the solution.

Note:

Deleting/Clearing transactions can result in data loss or incomplete processing. Ensure that the data being deleted is not required and/or can be sent from the sender system again.

1.4  RecoveryDo

Target: recovery the in-doubt transactions to Final status.

The integration framework tries to use RecoveryDo to recover the in-doubt transactions to bring them to Final status. Recovery is triggered on timer basis or a restart of the Integration Framework service. To trigger an immediate recovery, choose Integration Framework → TOOLS → Control Center → Maintenance → Schedule Recovery and click Submit.

 (Screenshot – 8: Schedule Recovery)

For some in-doubt transactions, a retry will not recover the transaction, which may lead to the RecoveryDo IPO Step being deactivated too. In the IPO Step Status, you may see the RecoveryDo is deactivated and the messages not processed in the queue of “_!!Recovery”. To solve such situations, try to resolve the in-doubt transactions, afterwards if the recovery is deactivated again, refer to the chapter 1.2.

1.5 Default Error Handling

Target: bypass the problematic message to improve the queue situation.

The integration framework provides the option to move the message from queue to message log, and to continue processing the next message.

Choose MAINTENANCE → Cfg Error Handling → Choose "Remove from queue and continue with next message".


Note: The message is moved to the message log failure section. The user needs to manually retry the message log/retrigger the replication.

 (Screenshot – 9: Cfg Error Handling)

 ﻿﻿2. General Process for troubleshooting

When the replication doesn’t work as expected/the receiver doesn’t get the output, follow the steps to troubleshoot:

2.1 Go to the Integration Framework → MONITORING → Message Log to check if there is any log related. In case there is no log related, continue to check the queue monitor.

In some cases, the message logs stay in Success section, however the replication doesn’t work. The message log should include the exception information which doesn’t cause queue blocking. For example, DI Proxy exception.

Note: In case of an event triggered scenario, ensure that the event is not filtered and sent to integration framework without issue. Otherwise there would be no message log. Refer to the SAP Note 1882000 to troubleshoot the event related issue.

2.2 Go to the Integration Framework → MONITORING → Queue Monitor to monitor the queue processing. When the relevant queue doesn’t process as expected for a long time or the message is removed from the queue but is sent back to the queue repeatedly, continue to check the IPO Step Status.

2.3 Go to the Integration Framework → TOOLS → Control Center → IPO Step Status to check the status. Refer to chapter 1.2 for the details. If no relevant IPO Step is deactivated, or in case the IPO Step deactivated again quickly after activating and refreshing, continue to check the TID-Panel.

2.4 Go to the Integration Framework → TOOLS → Control Center → TID Panel. Refer to chapter 1.3 for the details. Afterwards, go back to chapter 2.3 and if there is no IPO Step deactivated anymore, check the queue monitor again as stated in chapter 2.3. If the queue is still blocked, continue to chapter 2.5.

2.5 Refer to chapter 1.4 to check if the RecoveryDo is triggered but deactivated and blocks the queue.

2.6 In case the queue is continuously blocked after deleting the first message, refer to the chapter 1.5 to change the default error handling setting.

2.7 If the queue issue persists without processing at all by following all of the steps above, refer to the chapter 2.1 documented in the SAP Note 2151806 to check the database blocking.

2.8 Refer to the SAP Note 2192737 to check if the best practice is followed.

2.9 Continue to the chapter 4 to report the issue to SAP in case all of the troubleshooting steps don’t help.

﻿3. Useful Information

Case Study: Intercompany Performance and Troubleshooting

Integration framework > HELP > Online Help > Troubleshooting

SAP Note 1882000 - Troubleshooting SAP Business One Event Sender

SAP Note 2192737 - Best practices: Performance optimization of the Integration Framework

SAP Note 2130388 - Troubleshooting technical exceptions in Integration Framework

SAP Note 2360911 - How to troubleshoot DI errors and report SAP incidents

﻿4. Report an SAP incident

If you need assistance from SAP support, create an incident in SMP with component "SBO-INT-B1IF-SR" / "SBO-ICO" (for intercompany solution) and attach below information:

4.1 Run the integration framework troubleshooting tool when the blocking issue occurs and export the result as a zip file.

Choose TOOLS → Troubleshooting → Choose B1i Framework, click Submit, click the Start buttons, click Export Result, and select Export XML Result as ZIP.

4.2 Check the in-doubt transactions in the SQL Server Management Studio/HANA Studio. Run the following query in the IFSERV/B1iF database and export the result to a csv file.

SELECT 'STARTED' as "STATUS", * FROM "XCLTRLOG" WHERE "STATUS" = 0 AND "TSTAMP" > '20070101000000' ORDER BY "TSTAMP" DESC;
SELECT 'INCOMMIT' as "STATUS", * FROM "XCLTRLOG" WHERE "STATUS" = 1 AND "TSTAMP" > '20070101000000' ORDER BY "TSTAMP" DESC;
SELECT 'INROLLBACK' as "STATUS", * FROM "XCLTRLOG" WHERE "STATUS" = 5 AND "TSTAMP" > '20070101000000' ORDER BY "TSTAMP" DESC;

4.3 Provide a screenshot of the Queue Monitor. Refer to the chapter 1.1.

4.4 Provide a screenshot of the IPO Step Status. Refer to the chapter 1.2.

4.5 Provide a screenshot of the Cfg Error handling setting. Refer to the chapter 1.5.

4.6 Provide a screenshot of the RecoveryDo transaction list.

Choose TOOLS → Control Center → Monitoring → TID Panel, select the "/com_sap_b1i_system_xc/com.sap.b1i.system.xc/ipo/cronjobs.ipo/RecoveryDo" (IPO Step URI) and click Submit.

4.7 Provide the results of the database blocking detection as documented in the chapter 2.1 of the SAP Note 2151806.

4.8 Provide the Maximum memory pool allocated to B1i (tomcat): run ..\SAP\SAP Business One Integration\IntegrationServer\Tomcat\bin\tomcat6w.exe and find the field on Java tab.

4.9 Provide the scenario package if the issue is caused by the third-party scenario. Also provide the message in the Queue Monitor and/or message log in the Message Log.

4.10 Provide the IPO Log for the in-doubt transaction which is identified to be relevant during the troubleshooting from TOOLS → Control Center → Monitoring → TID Panel → ... → IPO Log → Open in XML → save the page as XML file. Refer to the chapter 1.3.

4.11 Provide the information of the troubleshooting steps you performed according to the chapter 1 and results.
